response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack software developer with strong web, mobile, and backend credentials, but lacks the core qualifications required for a Principal Performance Architect role focused on AI accelerators. No evidence of SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture work, or deep learning workload optimization. Education is a BS with ~14 years experience (meets the 8+ year bar), but the domain mismatch is severe.
strengths: Long track record (~14 years) of professional software development
Low-latency systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests/second
Some C/C++ exposure at National Instruments on measurement/instrumentation tools
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, or hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or deep-learning workload optimization background
Career is web/full-stack focused — not a performance/hardware architect profile

key_factors.factor: Domain mismatch with AI accelerator hardware architecture
Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware-software co-design skills
Meets years-of-experience threshold for BS path
